response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack/web engineer with strong software craftsmanship, but the role demands principal-level expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, and hardware-software co-design. Aside from some low-latency DLang services, brief C/C++ IDE tooling work, and a hobbyist audio/embedded project, there is no evidence of silicon modeling, GPU/SIMD architecture, or deep-learning workload optimization experience. The domain gap is too large for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: Meets the BS + 8+ years experience bar with a long, consistent engineering track record
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec with async I/O, fibers, caching)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and knowledge sharing via talks and workshops
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU/SIMD/MIMD workload analysis background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not performance architecture — a large domain pivot at principal level

key_factors.factor: Solid systems-level performance fundamentals (low-latency services, C/C++)
